questions.question: Is the storage-failure handling in the wipe helper a real try/catch around each of the two calls (or the pair), or a vacuous stub that swallows *all* errors including ones unrelated to storage (masking real bugs) or that never actually calls the real `clearMessages`/`clearDirectMessageContact` in the failure-path test (a mocked-away no-op that trivially 'doesn't throw')? Provide the helper's exact error-handling code and confirm the storage-quota test drives a real `clearMessages`/`clearDirectMessageContact` implementation that is made to reject (e.g. via a fake IDB quota error), not a pre-stubbed always-resolving mock.

Does this story confine itself to the 'chat persistence' module (`app/src/lib/marmot/chatPersistence.ts`, `app/src/lib/unreadStore.ts`) and export a single wipe-helper entry point rather than duplicating the two-call sequence and its failure handling elsewhere? Run `grep -rn 'clearMessages\|clearDirectMessageContact' app/src` and confirm the only place both calls appear together is the new/modified helper inside `chatPersistence.ts` — not inlined again at a future S4 call site.

Could 'chat persistence' (`chatPersistence.ts`, `unreadStore.ts`) be deleted and rewritten without modifying any file outside its dependencies_allowed list — with S4 (`profile.tsx#handleArchiveToggle`) calling only the exported wipe-helper entry point via the declared `HistoryWipeResult` seam, never reaching into `chatPersistence.ts` internals? If S4 would need to import an internal helper (not the public wipe entry point) to complete its block-branch orchestration, identify that dependency.

Do `app/tests/unit/chatPersistence-purge.test.ts`, `chatPersistence-property.test.ts`, and `unreadStore.test.ts` cover, as separate assertions: (a) the two-call sequence and post-wipe absence of the idb-keyval thread record (AC-WIPE-1), (b) unread-counter/last-read clearing (AC-WIPE-2), (c) `StoredContact` retention with `archivedAt` set (AC-WIPE-3), (d) the `directConversationId`-only key derivation with no hand-built literal (AC-WIPE-4), (e) the storage-failure no-throw/no-block-prevention case (AC-WIPE-5), and (f) the in-flight `appendMessage` drain race (AC-WIPE-6)? List the specific test names for each.

Does this story implement only the wipe helper itself, with the invocation from the block action (UI trigger, confirm dialog) explicitly deferred to S4, and with no change to `isAllowedDmSender`/`isBlockedPeer` (S1) or the notification watcher (S2)? Confirm the helper is exported but not yet called from any `profile.tsx` or `contacts.tsx` code path in this story's diff.

Does a thrown/rejected error from either `clearMessages` or `clearDirectMessageContact` get logged (not silently discarded) while still allowing the calling block action to complete and set `archivedAt`? Provide a test spying on the logging call (e.g. `console.error` or the project's logger) asserting it fires exactly once per simulated failure, and that the block action's caller receives no unhandled rejection.

Does `git diff --stat` for this story's commit(s) touch only files inside this story's declared scope (`app/src/lib/marmot/chatPersistence.ts`, `app/src/lib/unreadStore.ts`, the three named test files) or files already listed in `specs/epic-block-contact/epic-state.json#pre_existing_dirty_files`? Any other file appearing in the diff is a scope-boundary violation.

Does the block action call `clearMessages(directConversationId(peerPubkeyHex))` such that after a successful block, the idb-keyval record at key `few:messages:dm:<peerHexLower>` is absent (a `readIdbRecord` lookup on `keyval-store`/`keyval` returns null/undefined)? Provide (a) the call site, and (b) a test that writes a message, blocks the peer, and asserts the idb-keyval read returns nothing.

Does the block action call `clearDirectMessageContact(peerPubkeyHex)` such that after a successful block, the unread counter and last-read timestamp for that peer are cleared, and the notification-badge count attributable to that peer is zero? Provide a test that increments the peer's unread counter, blocks the peer, and asserts both the counter and last-read timestamp are cleared.

Does the wipe helper leave the `StoredContact` record for `peerPubkeyHex` intact after a block, with `archivedAt` set to a non-null timestamp and `readStoredContacts()` still returning that entry? Provide a test asserting the contact record survives the wipe with the correct `archivedAt` value — never deleted from `lp_contacts_v1`.

Does the wipe helper derive its storage key exclusively via `directConversationId(peerPubkeyHex)`, with no hand-built `dm:<peer>`-shaped string literal anywhere in this story's diff? Run `grep -n '`dm:\$' app/src/lib/marmot/chatPersistence.ts app/src/lib/unreadStore.ts` (and any other file this story touches) and confirm zero matches for a hand-built template-literal key.

If `clearMessages` or `clearDirectMessageContact` throws (simulated storage-quota failure), does the block action still set `archivedAt` on the contact record, does the composite gate still return `false` for that peer immediately afterward, and does the wipe failure neither prevent the block's filtering effect nor throw out of the block action? Provide a test that forces each of the two calls to reject in turn and asserts, for both cases, `archivedAt` is set and the composite gate denies the peer, with no exception propagating to the caller.

Does an `appendMessage` write in flight for `dm:<peer>` at the moment block is triggered fail to resurrect the thread key after `clearMessages` completes — i.e. does the idb-keyval record for that thread remain absent even if the in-flight write resolves after the clear call started? Provide a test that starts an `appendMessage` promise, triggers the block/wipe before it resolves, lets both settle, and asserts the thread key is absent regardless of resolution order.

Contract check for the `HistoryWipeResult` seam this story defines (consumed by S4's `handleArchiveToggle` block branch): does the wipe helper never throw out of the block action even when either underlying call rejects, does it drain any in-flight `appendMessage` write for the peer's thread before completing (per AC-WIPE-6), and does it derive its storage key exclusively via `directConversationId(peerHex)` (per AC-WIPE-4), never a hand-built `dm:<peer>` literal? Provide one test per invariant (three total) demonstrating S4 can rely on this contract without inspecting the helper's internals.

Implementation created a NEW test file, `app/tests/unit/chatPersistence-wipe.test.ts`, rather than adding to the three test files named in the story's original scope notes (`chatPersistence-purge.test.ts`, `chatPersistence-property.test.ts`, `unreadStore.test.ts`). Is this an acceptable scope interpretation — the file is chat-persistence-scoped, follows the same idb-keyval/localStorage mocking conventions as `chatPersistence-purge.test.ts`, and mirrors the precedent set by S2's `directMessageNotifications-block-suppression.test.ts` (a new file, not an addition to a pre-existing one)? Confirm no test coverage for `wipeSinglePeerHistory`/`HistoryWipeResult` exists anywhere else that would make this a duplicate.

The AC-WIPE-5 test for a `clearDirectMessageContact` failure forces the throw via `vi.mock('@/src/lib/unreadStore', importOriginal)` wrapping the real export in `vi.fn(actual.clearDirectMessageContact)` and then `mockImplementationOnce(() => { throw ... })` for that one test. Does this genuinely exercise `wipeSinglePeerHistory`'s own try/catch around a real underlying call in every OTHER test in the file (i.e., is the wrapped mock a pass-through to the real implementation by default, not a permanently stubbed no-op), and does `getDirectMessageLastReadAt(PEER_HEX)` in the 'still runs the counters clear' scenario reflect a real `clearMessages` execution rather than a mocked idb-keyval short-circuit?

`wipeSinglePeerHistory` imports `clearDirectMessageContact` from `@/src/lib/unreadStore` as a STATIC top-level import (not the dynamic `import()` pattern `clearMessages` already uses for its `messageEdits/api.ts` aux-cleanup). Was this verified safe against circular-import risk — i.e. does `unreadStore.ts` import anything from `chatPersistence.ts` (directly or transitively), which would make the static edge introduce a cycle? Confirm via `grep -n "chatPersistence" app/src/lib/unreadStore.ts` returning zero matches, and note that `directMessages.ts`'s existing references to `chatPersistence.ts` are all dynamic `import()` calls, so `chatPersistence.ts`'s pre-existing static import of `directConversationId` from `directMessages.ts` was already cycle-safe before this story added the `unreadStore.ts` edge.
